SEARCH
Package com.comp2042.service.audio

Class SoundManager

java.lang.Object
com.comp2042.service.audio.SoundManager
public class SoundManager extends Object
Centralized audio manager for sound effects and background music (Singleton Pattern). Uses JavaFX Media API with performance optimizations (throttling, caching, proper disposal). Supports mode-specific BGM: Main Menu, Endless, Two-Player, and Level-specific tracks.
Author:
Dong, Jia.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static SoundManager getInstance()
      Gets the singleton instance (thread-safe lazy initialization).
      Returns:
      SoundManager instance

    • playAttackSound

      public void playAttackSound()
      Plays an attack sound effect (for Two-Player Mode).

    • playAttackReceivedSound

      public void playAttackReceivedSound()
      Plays a sound effect when receiving an attack (for Two-Player Mode).

    • playLineClearSound

      public void playLineClearSound(int linesCleared)
      Plays a line clear sound effect based on the number of lines cleared. Uses different sound effects for 1, 2, 3, or 4 lines cleared.
      Parameters:
      linesCleared - the number of lines cleared (1-4)

    • playLineClearSound

      public void playLineClearSound()
      Plays a line clear sound effect (default - single line). Maintains backward compatibility.

      Note: Reserved for backward compatibility - not currently invoked

    • playComboSound

      public void playComboSound(int comboCount)
      Plays a combo sound effect.
      Parameters:
      comboCount - the combo count

      Note: Parameter reserved for future use - currently plays same sound for all combos

    • playCountdownSound

      public void playCountdownSound()
      Plays a game start countdown sound. Uses a dedicated MediaPlayer to prevent duplicate playback.

    • stopCountdownSound

      public void stopCountdownSound()
      Stops the countdown sound if it's currently playing.

    • playGameStartSound

      public void playGameStartSound()
      Plays a game start sound.

    • playSoftDropSound

      public void playSoftDropSound()
      Plays a soft drop sound effect. Uses throttling to prevent too frequent playback during rapid soft drops.

    • playHardDropSound

      public void playHardDropSound()
      Plays a hard drop sound effect.

    • playHoldSound

      public void playHoldSound()
      Plays a hold brick sound effect.

    • playMoveSound

      public void playMoveSound()
      Plays a move brick sound effect. Uses throttling to prevent too frequent playback during rapid movements.

    • playRotateSound

      public void playRotateSound()
      Plays a rotate brick sound effect. Uses throttling to prevent too frequent playback during rapid rotations.

    • playPauseResumeSound

      public void playPauseResumeSound()
      Plays a pause/resume sound effect.

    • playGarbageLinesSound

      public void playGarbageLinesSound()
      Plays garbage lines sound effect (for Two-Player Mode).

    • playWarningGarbageSound

      public void playWarningGarbageSound()
      Plays a warning garbage sound effect (for Two-Player Mode).

    • playLevelWinSound

      public void playLevelWinSound()
      Plays a level win sound effect.

    • playLevelFailedSound

      public void playLevelFailedSound()
      Plays a level failed sound effect.

    • playGameOverSound

      public void playGameOverSound()
      Plays a game over sound effect. Used for general game over scenarios.

    • playEndlessGameOverSound

      public void playEndlessGameOverSound()
      Plays an endless mode game over sound effect. Used specifically for endless mode game over.

    • playEndlessNewRecordSound

      public void playEndlessNewRecordSound()
      Plays an endless mode new record sound effect. Used when a new high score is achieved in endless mode.

    • playTwoPlayerGameOverSound

      public void playTwoPlayerGameOverSound()
      Plays a two-player mode game over sound effect. Used when Two-Player Mode game ends.

    • playButtonClickSound

      public void playButtonClickSound()
      Plays a button click sound effect.

    • playMainMenuBackgroundMusic

      public void playMainMenuBackgroundMusic()
      Starts playing main menu background music in a loop. Used when the main menu is displayed.

    • playEndlessBackgroundMusic

      public void playEndlessBackgroundMusic()
      Starts playing endless mode background music in a loop. Used when entering endless mode.

    • playTwoPlayerBackgroundMusic

      public void playTwoPlayerBackgroundMusic()
      Starts playing two-player mode background music in a loop. Used when entering Two-Player Mode.

    • playBackgroundMusic

      public void playBackgroundMusic()
      Starts playing background music in a loop. Maintains backward compatibility - defaults to main menu music.

    • playLevelBackgroundMusic

      public void playLevelBackgroundMusic(int levelId)
      Starts playing level background music in a loop based on level number. Used when entering level mode.

      Music mapping:

      • Level 1, 3 & 5: Level135BGM.mp3
      • Level 2 & 4: Level24BGM.mp3
      Parameters:
      levelId - the level ID (1-5)

    • playLevelBackgroundMusic

      public void playLevelBackgroundMusic()
      Starts playing level background music in a loop. Defaults to Level135BGM (level 1 music) for backward compatibility.

      Note: Reserved for backward compatibility - not currently invoked

    • stopBackgroundMusic

      public void stopBackgroundMusic()
      Stops the background music.

    • pauseBackgroundMusic

      public void pauseBackgroundMusic()
      Pauses the background music.

      Note: Reserved for future use - not currently invoked

    • resumeBackgroundMusic

      public void resumeBackgroundMusic()
      Resumes the background music.

    • setSoundEnabled

      public void setSoundEnabled(boolean enabled)
      Sets whether sounds are enabled.
      Parameters:
      enabled - true to enable sounds, false to disable

      Note: Reserved for future use - not currently invoked

    • isSoundEnabled

      public boolean isSoundEnabled()
      Gets whether sounds are enabled.
      Returns:
      true if sounds are enabled, false otherwise

      Note: Reserved for future use - not currently invoked

    • setMasterVolume

      public void setMasterVolume(double masterVolume)
      Sets the master volume level (affects all audio).
      Parameters:
      masterVolume - master volume level (0.0 to 1.0)

    • getMasterVolume

      public double getMasterVolume()
      Gets the master volume level.
      Returns:
      master volume level (0.0 to 1.0)

      Note: Reserved for future use - not currently invoked. Application uses GameSettings.getMasterVolume() as the source of truth.

    • setSfxVolume

      public void setSfxVolume(double sfxVolume)
      Sets the volume level for sound effects (before master volume).
      Parameters:
      sfxVolume - sound effects volume level (0.0 to 1.0)

    • getSfxVolume

      public double getSfxVolume()
      Gets the volume level for sound effects (before master volume).
      Returns:
      sound effects volume level (0.0 to 1.0)

      Note: Reserved for future use - not currently invoked. Application uses GameSettings.getSfxVolume() as the source of truth.

    • setMusicVolume

      public void setMusicVolume(double musicVolume)
      Sets the volume level for background music (before master volume).
      Parameters:
      musicVolume - music volume level (0.0 to 1.0)

    • getMusicVolume

      public double getMusicVolume()
      Gets the volume level for background music (before master volume).
      Returns:
      music volume level (0.0 to 1.0)

      Note: Reserved for future use - not currently invoked. Application uses GameSettings.getMusicVolume() as the source of truth.

    • setVolumes

      public void setVolumes(double masterVolume, double musicVolume, double sfxVolume)
      Sets all volume levels at once from GameSettings. This is the recommended way to update volumes from settings.
      Parameters:
      masterVolume - master volume level (0.0 to 1.0)
      musicVolume - music volume level (0.0 to 1.0)
      sfxVolume - sound effects volume level (0.0 to 1.0)
